rights: You have the right to know the exact reason you are being summoned.
You may be represented by a lawyer in court.
deadlines: You must appear before the court on the exact date and time mentioned in the summons.
warnings: Failure to appear can result in an arrest warrant being issued against you.
Do not ignore the summons under any circumstances.
facts: A summons is a legal order issued by a court directing an individual to appear before a judge.

rights: You have the right to get a free copy of the FIR.
You have the right to seek anticipatory bail if you fear arrest.
warnings: Do not tamper with any evidence or threaten witnesses.
facts: An FIR (First Information Report) is the first step in a criminal investigation.

rights: You have the right to reply to the legal notice through your advocate.
You have the right to demand proof of the claims made in the notice.
deadlines: You must reply to the notice within the stipulated time frame (usually 15-30 days).
warnings: Ignoring a legal notice may result in the sender initiating civil or criminal proceedings against you in court.
facts: A legal notice is a formal communication to a person or an entity, informing them that the sender intends to undertake legal proceedings against them.

rights: You have the right to be released from custody once the bail conditions are met and sureties are accepted.
deadlines: You must present yourself at the police station or court exactly as directed by the bail conditions.
warnings: Violating any condition of the bail (like travelling abroad without permission) can lead to immediate cancellation of your bail and re-arrest.
facts: Bail allows an accused person to remain free until their trial is completed, subject to specific restrictions.
